_comment: The mis-tuned-boundary control for echelon-battle.json (docs/large-scale-simulation-design.md, phase 4; see tools/benchmark/README.md, 'Tuning the tier thresholds'). Identical army to echelon-battle.json, but the team-0 second echelon sits 500 world units behind the front -- inside the (PROMOTE_RANGE, DEMOTE_RANGE) hysteresis band, so the reserves keep their spawn tier (close) and never demote. All ~1,990 soldiers then run per-soldier simulation for the whole window, standing in for what a demote threshold looser than the echelon depth would cost: the run measurably exceeds the 60fps tick budget that echelon-battle.json (same army, reserves aggregate) stays inside. The pair turns 'the thresholds hold the promoted bubble under the measured ceiling' into two reproducible numbers.
